title: T-013 — Seed lp_state_machine_v1 DSL rule (WH §6.1, ADR-029)
prompt:
# T-013 — Seed lp_state_machine_v1 DSL rule (WH §6.1, ADR-029)

PRD: docs/prd/05-WAREHOUSE-PRD.md §6.1, §16.5
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Seed the `lp_state_machine_v1` rule into 02-SETTINGS rule registry, encoding the §6.1 state machine (available ⇄ reserved → consumed; available → blocked → available; merge / shipped paths) with per-transition guards and allowed reason_codes. Admin read-only per ADR-029.

## Implementation contract
1. Add seed file `packages/db/src/seeds/lp-state-machine-v1.ts` producing an INSERT/UPSERT into the rule_registry table (owned by 02-SETTINGS §7) with rule_id='lp_state_machine_v1', version=1, kind='state_machine', payload (JSON DSL encoding §6.1 transitions).
2. Each transition record: `{from, to, guards: [...], allowed_reasons: [...]}` per §6.1 table.
3. Server-side helper `getAllowedTransitions(from_state): Transition[]` reads from rule_registry, used by T-019 service.
4. Admin UI is read-only (no edit endpoint in this task — ADR-029).

## Files
- packages/db/seed/rules/lp-state-machine-v1.json [create]
- packages/db/seed/rules/lp-state-machine-v1.seed.ts [create]
- packages/db/seed/rules/__tests__/lp-state-machine-v1.test.ts [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given the seed runs, when rule_registry is queried for `rule_id='lp_state_machine_v1'`, then it returns exactly one row with version=1 and the JSON payload includes every §6.1 transition.
2. Given `getAllowedTransitions('available')` is called, when inspected, then it returns ['reserved','blocked','consumed','merged','shipped'] (per §6.1).
3. Given a destructive transition (e.g., available→blocked), when its allowed_reasons are queried, then reason_code is required and the list matches §6.1.

## Test strategy
- RED: `packages/db/src/seeds/__tests__/lp-state-machine-v1.test.ts` asserting payload shape + `getAllowedTransitions` semantics.
- Run `pnpm --filter db test lp-state-machine-v1`.

## Risk red lines
- Do not add an admin edit endpoint — admins are read-only per ADR-029 (changes go through PR/deploy).
- Do not hardcode transitions in the state-transition service — they MUST come from the registry.
- Do not omit `allowed_reasons` per transition (drives V-WH-LP-010).

## Dependencies
- T-001
